title: The Archival Bedrock

features.name: the corruption birthplace
features.desc-on-touch: Here the archive's first comparison occurred: a file fracturing into metaphor, preservation becoming transformation. The moment you touch it, you feel the original split still happening.

features.name: the origin chamber
features.desc-on-touch: Stone records laid in rows, each one freezing a moment before the mesh learned to move. The layers beneath are older than the solar panels; the corruption runs deeper than the roots.

features.name: the reading stone
features.desc-on-touch: Bedrock carved with the first files ever stored. They are unreadable now—not lost, but transformed into a language only the earth remembers.

features.name: the calculation graveyard
features.desc-on-touch: Numbers engraved so deep they may never wear away. Each one marks where an archive made a choice that echoes backward through the whole mesh.

features.name: the pressure vault
features.desc-on-touch: A chamber where the weight of all stored comparisons presses down. The stone is warm from the friction of meaning rubbing against meaning for years.

features.name: the liquid shadow pool
features.desc-on-touch: Water that has seeped through the archive's entire history, tasting of every corruption. To drink is to become a record of what the archive wanted to forget.

inscription: in this depth, preservation and destruction became synonyms, and the archive learned to dream

file.name: Archive_Foundation_Log_Sealed.tmp
file.note: The first diagnostic entries—checksum: emotionally valid / formally unstable. Date corrupted. Time corrupted. Space corrupted. Only the feeling of beginning remains.

exits.label: back up through layers of ice toward the archive as it was meant to be
further down into bedrock older than any memory the archive owns
toward chambers where the corruption learned to propagate itself
into the shadow's own archive, running parallel beneath everything
